questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Direct current (DC) cannot flow through a capacitor because a capacitor blocks the flow of steady-state currents. Alternating current (AC) can flow through a capacitor because it continually changes direction, allowing charges to alternately accumulate and discharge on the capacitor plates. The capacitor charges and discharges in sync with the AC cycle, facilitating the flow of alternating current.

In free space (a vacuum), D and E are directly proportional with a constant of proportionality equal to ε₀.Deriving the unit:From the equation D = ε₀ * E, we can see that:D has units of C/m² (charge per unit area).E has units of N/C (force per unit charge).Solving for ε₀:ε₀ = D / E = (C/m²) / (N/C)Performing the division, we get:ε₀ = C² / (N m²)Incorrect options: (a), (b), and (c) These units do not correctly represent the SI unit of permittivity of free space.
